senses.definition: Delayed something so that it will happen at a later time or date.
examples: The concert was postponed because of the storm.
They postponed the meeting until next Monday.

senses.definition: A decision to delay an event or action until a later time or date.
examples: We were upset by the postponement of our flight.
The company announced the postponement of the product launch.

senses.definition: More than one case of an event or action being delayed until a later time or date.
examples: Several postponements of the trial frustrated the victims.
After a series of postponements, the match finally went ahead.

senses.definition: The act of considering something less important or of lower rank than something else, now only used historically.
examples: In older philosophical texts, postponence of pleasure to duty is often praised.
The term "postponence" appears in some eighteenth‑century writings.

senses.definition: Plural of postponence, meaning several acts of treating something as less important or lower in rank, used only historically.
examples: The author listed various postponences of personal interest in favor of public good.

senses.definition: A person or thing that postpones something.
examples: The constant postponer of decisions can slow down a project.
She jokingly called herself a postponer rather than a procrastinator.

senses.definition: People or things that often postpone events, tasks, or decisions.
examples: Chronic postponers may struggle to meet deadlines.
Postponers in the team created frustration for those who wanted quick action.

senses.definition: Delays something so that it will happen at a later time or date.
examples: The committee often postpones difficult decisions.
If it rains, the club usually postpones the match.

senses.definition: Delaying something so that it happens at a later time or date.
examples: We are considering postponing the conference.
Postponing the repairs could make the problem worse.

senses.definition: Located or occurring behind the pons, a part of the brainstem.
examples: The scan showed a lesion in the postpontile region.
Postpontile structures can be affected by certain brainstem strokes.

senses.definition: To place a word or phrase after another word, especially in grammar.
examples: In some languages, adjectives are often postposed after the noun.
The editor decided to postpose the clause for stylistic reasons.

senses.definition: Placed a word or phrase after another word, especially in grammar.
examples: In this construction, the adjective is postposed after the noun.
The relative clause was postposed to avoid ambiguity.

senses.definition: Places a word or phrase after another word, especially in grammar.
examples: The language regularly postposes adjectives after nouns.
This structure postposes the prepositional phrase.

senses.definition: Placing a word or phrase after another word, especially in grammar.
examples: Postposing the adjective can give it extra emphasis.
In some languages, postposing the verb is common in questions.

senses.definition: To place after something else, especially to place a word after another word in grammar.
examples: Some languages postposit articles after nouns.
The editor chose to postposit the phrase for clarity.

senses.definition: Placed after something else, especially when a word is placed after another word in grammar.
examples: Here the preposition is postposited after its object.
The adjective is postposited rather than placed before the noun.

senses.definition: A word, similar to a preposition, that is placed after the noun or pronoun it relates to, especially in some languages.
examples: In Japanese, many relationships are shown by using a postposition after the noun.
The language uses a postposition instead of a preposition in this phrase.

senses.definition: The act of placing something after something else.
examples: Postposition of the clause made the sentence sound more natural.
The style guide allows postposition of certain phrases.

senses.definition: Relating to or using postpositions in grammar.
examples: Finnish has many postpositional phrases.
In a postpositional language, these function words follow the noun.

senses.definition: In a way that involves being placed after another word, especially as a postposition.
examples: The particle is used postpositionally in this structure.
In this language, case markers function postpositionally.

senses.definition: Words, similar to prepositions, that are placed after the noun or pronoun they relate to.
examples: Japanese uses many postpositions to show grammatical relationships.
Students compared the use of postpositions in different languages.

senses.definition: Placed after the word it modifies, especially an adjective coming after a noun.
examples: In the phrase "attorney general", the adjective is postpositive.
Certain titles include postpositive adjectives for historical reasons.

senses.definition: In a way that places a word after the word it modifies.
examples: The title uses the adjective postpositively.
In this structure, the modifier appears postpositively for emphasis.

senses.definition: Words, especially adjectives, that come after the words they modify.
examples: Learners sometimes find postpositives in legal phrases confusing.
The style guide includes a list of common postpositives.

senses.definition: Happening after a meal.
examples: The doctor measured her postprandial blood sugar level.
They took a postprandial walk in the park.

senses.definition: In a way that occurs after a meal.
examples: Insulin levels rise postprandially.
She often feels sleepy postprandially.

senses.definition: In traditional logic, one of five extra categories: opposition, priority, simultaneity, movement, or possession, discussed after the main categories.
examples: The philosopher briefly mentioned the postpredicament of movement.
Students struggled to understand how postpredicaments fit into Aristotle's system.

senses.definition: Relating to the period after someone has finished being president.
examples: Her postpresidential years were filled with charity work.
The book examines the president's postpresidential influence on policy.

senses.definition: Relating to the period or education level after primary school.
examples: The report focuses on postprimary education in rural areas.
Many students drop out before entering postprimary levels.

senses.definition: Happening after a person has been released from prison.
examples: He struggled to find a job in his postprison life.
The program offers support during the postprison adjustment period.

senses.definition: In the time after release from prison.
examples: He remained under supervision postprison.
Her situation improved postprison.

senses.definition: To treat data, images, or other outputs after they have been produced, especially by a computer program or camera.
examples: You can postprocess the photos to correct the color balance.
The software allows you to postprocess the raw data.

senses.definition: The activity of changing or improving data, images, or other outputs after they have been produced.
examples: The film still needs some postprocessing before release.
Image postprocessing improved the clarity of the results.

senses.definition: A tool or program that processes data, images, or results after they have been produced.
examples: The simulation uses a separate postprocessor to generate reports.
An image postprocessor can remove noise from photos.

senses.definition: The work done on a film, recording, or video after the main shooting or recording is finished, such as editing and adding sound.
examples: The movie is now in postproduction.
Good postproduction can greatly improve a low‑budget film.

senses.definition: Different sets of work on films, recordings, or videos after the main shooting or recording is finished.
examples: The company manages postproductions for several major studios.
These two postproductions were handled by different teams.

senses.definition: To interpret or speak about an event as if it had been predicted, but only after it has already happened, often humorously or critically.
examples: Commentators tend to postprophesy after the match is over.
It is easy to postprophesy the crisis once you know the outcome.

senses.definition: Relating to a period after a prophecy has been made or after a recognized prophetic age.
examples: The text reflects a postprophetic understanding of history.
Their faith developed in a postprophetic community.

senses.definition: Another form of postprophetic, relating to the time after prophetic messages or figures.
examples: The book explores postprophetical religious movements.
Postprophetical literature often reinterprets earlier prophecies.

senses.definition: Occurring after treatment or removal of the prostate gland.
examples: The patient reported postprostate urinary problems.
Postprostate care focuses on managing side effects.

senses.definition: Relating to theories, methods, or periods that come after classical psychoanalysis and respond to or develop it.
examples: The course examines postpsychoanalytic approaches to therapy.
Her work is rooted in postpsychoanalytic feminist theory.

senses.definition: Relating to the time after puberty has been completed.
examples: The study focused on postpubertal boys.
Certain conditions appear only in the postpubertal period.

senses.definition: The period of life after a person has gone through puberty.
examples: Certain hormonal changes continue well into postpuberty.
The researchers compared prepuberty and postpuberty behavior.

senses.definition: Having passed through puberty.
examples: The clinic provides services for postpubescent adolescents.
Postpubescent individuals may face new emotional challenges.

senses.definition: People, especially young people, who have passed through puberty.
examples: The study looked at the diet of postpubescents.
Postpubescents often experience rapid emotional changes.

senses.definition: Located behind the pubic bone.
examples: The surgeon noted a mass in the postpubic area.
Postpubic pain can be a sign of certain pelvic conditions.

senses.definition: In some animals, the part of the pubic bone that points backward behind the main body of the pelvis.
examples: The dinosaur's postpubis pointed toward the tail.
Researchers examined the shape of the postpubis in different species.

senses.definition: Occurring after the period just after childbirth.
examples: The condition developed in the postpuerperal phase.
Postpuerperal depression can appear weeks after delivery.

senses.definition: Occurring after a pulmonary event or related to the area beyond the pulmonary region.
examples: Patients can suffer postpulmonary complications after a severe embolism.
The study described postpulmonary effects on exercise capacity.

senses.definition: A style of rock music that developed after punk rock, often more experimental and artistic.
examples: She listens mainly to postpunk from the late 1970s.
The band mixes postpunk with electronic sounds.

senses.definition: Relating to postpunk music or style.
examples: They created a dark, postpunk atmosphere on the album.
Her postpunk look includes black clothes and sharp hairstyles.

senses.definition: Located behind the pupil of the eye.
examples: The lens sits in a postpupillary position.
Postpupillary opacities can affect vision.

senses.definition: Relating to the stage after pyknosis, when a cell nucleus has already shrunk and is breaking apart.
examples: Postpycnotic cells were identified in the tissue sample.
The study focused on postpycnotic changes in the neurons.
